patterns_recognized: Away-team pick-direction error resolved: Prior slates (6/2, 6/1, 5/29-30) showed Claude Brain reading p_model as home-team probability and misidentifying away picks. All 15 today verified from CSV `pick` column directly — KC (away at CIN), LAD (away at ARI) both correctly read.

HARD CAP 9 pipeline miss on TOR@ATL: pick_prob=0.8185 exceeds the >0.80 SKIP threshold (archive: 33.3% actual vs 87.3% predicted in that bucket) but HARD CAP 9 is absent from grade_reasons. Enforcing SKIP manually.

D-grade dual-signal-against (SP xERA + PQI both oppose pick) appears four times today: CHW@MIN, CLE@NYY, OAK@CHC, PIT@HOU. All correctly SKIP. Per 20-day archive, this archetype fails directionally 60-70% of the time — treat as expected wrong direction, not neutral.

PLATINUM tier 43% historical hit rate: four PLATINUM labels today (MIA@WSH, TOR@ATL, COL@LAA, LAD@ARI). Two already capped to SKIP by HARD CAP 1+10; one manually enforced to SKIP (TOR@ATL); one downgraded to GOLD (MIA@WSH). None survive at PLATINUM conviction.

Consecutive-day same-matchup context: COL beat LAA 8-2 on 6/2 (LAA SP Sugano K%=12.92% blowout loss); today COL@LAA with Ureña (21.4% K%) and negative edge. Series carry-over risk noted but caps fire independently. TEX beat STL 7-4 on 6/2; today TEX@STL with Gore (25.09% K%) vs Pallante (18.33%) and carry thesis hampered by near-zero bullpen gap.

MIA @ WSH.reasoning: WSH PLATINUM carry thesis lost 7-3 on 6/1 with identical f5_full_delta=0.303 structure (Alcantara dominated; bullpen advantage was irrelevant). Today MIA sends Max Meyer (not Alcantara) so WSH direction remains defensible: bullpen gap=-0.0121 and F2+F3 dual-signal support Stage-2 carry. However, PLATINUM 43% historical hit rate, PQI against pick (-9.4), SP edge unconfirmed, and MIA's balanced lineup (concentration=1.185) punishing relievers cap conviction. Reduce to GOLD.

DET @ TB.reasoning: Edge=+2.52pp outside the [4,15]pp valid band; acute_roster and confidence_downgrade=True both flag elevated uncertainty. f5_full_delta=0.360 is the largest Stage-1/2 gap on the slate, meaning TB's full-game thesis is entirely a late-carry play with no verified bullpen rest. On 6/1 DET outlasted TB 10-9 in this same series (TB led early, DET rallied late). Multiple independent SKIP gates fire; no override basis.

CHW @ MIN.reasoning: D-grade (-2) with Taj Bradley (MIN, 27.49% K%) holding a 0.83 xERA edge over Erick Fedde (CHW, 14.41% K%) — a large K-rate differential confirming the xERA gap — AND PQI against CHW (Δ=+7.6). fair_prob=0.392 fails the 0.42 minimum gate; edge=+15.76pp outside valid band. Textbook D-grade dual-signal-against archetype; CHW direction is structurally suspect per the 20-day archive record.

NYM @ SEA.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 (edge=-6.85pp) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.522, archive non-CONFIRM hit rate 31.2%) both fire, reducing score from 3 to 0. Pre-cap conviction (F3+PQI+Stage agree) was real but market already prices SEA at 59.07% fair_prob vs model's 52.22% — market has the edge information. Per established doctrine, staking at inferior odds is negative EV. No qualitative basis to override either cap.

SD @ PHI.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 (edge=-12.61pp) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.506 bottom-bucket) both fire. F1_xera_gap=2.71 (Sanchez over Buehler) and PQI=+28.3 are strong directional signals but the market's -12.6pp disagreement is decisive — fair_prob=0.632 prices PHI at 63.2% vs model's 50.6%. Both caps fire on independent grounds; no qualitative basis to override.

BAL @ BOS.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 (edge=-5.37pp) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.509) fire. Tolle (BOS home SP, 26.97% K%) holds a 2.44 xERA edge and PQI=+34.9 strongly favors BOS, but f5_full_delta=0.194 means BOS full-game thesis is a carry play — and hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0011 (effectively zero, BOS pen not meaningfully better). Bullpen carry without a real gap is the 5/30 LAA@TB and 5/20 BOS@KC documented failure mode. SKIP correct.

CLE @ NYY.reasoning: D-grade (-1): Gavin Williams (CLE away, 27.95% K%) holds a 0.79 xERA edge over Gerrit Cole (NYY, 24.03% K%) AND PQI is strongly against NYY (Δ=-14.5). HARD CAP 1 (edge=-8.86pp) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.509) both fire. D-grade dual-signal-against on a PLATINUM label with multiple SKIP triggers; zero directional conviction on NYY per the archive's 20-day pattern record.

KC @ CIN.reasoning: Strongest pre-cap conviction on the slate (score=5, A-grade): F3_swing_take_gap=4291.8, SP xERA +1.47 (Kolek over Paddack), PQI confirming KC (Δ=-19.2), Stage 1/2 agree (Δ=0.02), edge=8.94pp in band, hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0027. HARD CAP 10 fires without CONFIRM; this CONFIRM rescues pre-cap A conviction. Archive: CONFIRM rescues in (0.50, 0.55] hit 47.6% vs 31.2% non-CONFIRM. Treat as low-conviction stake-down candidate only.

TOR @ ATL.reasoning: HARD CAP 9 should have fired (pick_prob=0.8185 > 0.80) but is absent from grade_reasons — pipeline miss. The >0.80 bucket shows catastrophic miscalibration (33.3% actual vs 87.3% predicted per archive audit). F1_xera_gap=1.22* carries a thin-sample asterisk; HARD CAP 7 already demoted score 3→1 (ATL pen gap=+0.0049). Edge=19.7pp sits in SOFT CAP 6.5 half-Kelly caution band. Enforcing HARD CAP 9 manually: DOWNGRADE to SKIP.

TEX @ STL.reasoning: Pre-cap score=3 (B+) with F3_swing_take_gap=1693.9 and PQI confirming STL (Δ=+7.8). HARD CAP 7 fired at hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0010 — economically negligible (third sub-+0.002 near-zero over-fire in the archive). f5_full_delta=0.2151 signals carry reliance, but STL direction is structurally sound. Edge=15.20pp is just above the [4,15]pp ceiling so stake_mult=0 regardless; no financial exposure either way.

OAK @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade (-2): Jeffrey Springs (OAK away, 21.33% K%) holds a 1.27 xERA edge over Colin Rea (CHC, 19.34% K%) AND PQI is strongly against CHC (Δ=-15.2). Negative edge (-1.11pp) fires HARD CAP 1. confidence_downgrade=True adds a secondary flag. All independent signals oppose CHC; textbook D-grade dual-signal-against with zero basis for directional confidence in CHC.

PIT @ HOU.reasoning: D-grade (-2): Paul Skenes (PIT away, 29.41% K%) holds a 2.40 xERA edge over Spencer Arrighetti (HOU, 21.66% K%) — one of the largest SP gaps on the slate — AND PQI is the most extreme negative on the entire slate (Δ=-41.0). Both primary signals oppose HOU by dominant margins. Per 5/28 archive, Skenes overcame even elite-level opposing signals in CHC@PIT (F1=4.25 lost); today HOU is not even the favorite structurally.

COL @ LAA.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 (edge=-6.11pp) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.518 bottom-bucket) both fire. F1_xera_gap=3.39 and PQI=+30.6 provide strong direction, but the market's -6.11pp disagreement is authoritative. f5_full_delta=0.247 adds carry-thesis risk. Context: COL beat LAA 8-2 yesterday (6/2) with a similar SP mismatch — consecutive-day rebound risk adds further caution. Both caps fire on independent grounds.

LAD @ ARI.reasoning: HARD CAP 10 fires (pick_prob=0.5031 — essentially a coin-flip). Ohtani pitching explains the extreme F5 dominance (LAD wins F5 at 84.76%) but full game closes to 50/50 because LAD's bullpen is WORSE than ARI's (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0059). Market already prices the Ohtani start at edge=-14.14pp (ARI favored). Pre-cap score was only 1 (B-); f5_full_delta=0.344 is extreme. Market on Ohtani starts is well-informed; SKIP is correct.

SF @ MIL.reasoning: MIL home SP not announced; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol is a hard stop. Grading requires 100+ Statcast pitches from both announced starters. Archive validates this repeatedly across 5/26, 5/29, 6/1 — thin-SP games produce blowouts in both directions with no reliable forecast. No directional inference warranted until MIL SP is confirmed and scoreable.
